doom.driver: Dramatic video shows the moment a U.S. fighter jet narrowly dodges an Iranian missile. Dramatic video shows the moment a missile came dangerously close to shooting down a F-18 fighter jet over Iran. It comes as Israel announced it killed Iran's navy chief.
doom.summary: A U.S. F-18 nearly became a very expensive fireball over Iranian airspace, while Israel quietly assassinated Iran's navy chief — you know, as one does during a 'deadline extension.' Meanwhile Trump says Iran let 10 oil tankers through as a 'present,' which is either diplomacy or the most elaborate hostage negotiation in history. Brent crude at $110, jet fuel up 106%, and Rubio is in Paris asking allies to please clap.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Active U.S.-Iran war with near-miss jet incident, Iran navy chief killed, and Trump extending deadlines while denying direct talks are happening — de-escalation rhetoric masking kinetic reality.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Senate finally funds most of DHS after 40-day shutdown, but excludes ICE — a partial fix that resolves airport chaos while leaving the core political fight intact.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Deepfake X-rays now fool radiologists at alarming rates, and a $500 GPU reportedly outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks — quiet capability creep on multiple fronts.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Global energy crisis deepens as Strait of Hormuz disruption hammers Southeast Asia, India slashes fuel taxes, and jet fuel doubles — economic contagion spreading well beyond the conflict zone.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Study finds sycophantic AI undermines human judgment, which is either a minor research note or a preview of civilization's final UX problem — but today it's just a study.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, night scene over the Persian Gulf — the view from directly behind and below a twin-tailed F/A-18 Super Hornet caught in a violent banking evasive roll, one wing nearly vertical, wingtip vapor cones glowing deep crimson against the black sky, the aircraft's twin engines burning amber exhaust trails that streak diagonally across the frame. The jet dominates the upper frame, its low-visibility grey fuselage lit from below by the hellish glow of a long white-grey missile body with a blazing orange rocket motor, the projectile impossibly close, its thick corkscrew smoke trail coiling through the darkness like a burning rope. The cockpit interior pulses with harsh green HUD symbology — flashing warning boxes, altitude digits, a horizon line tilted at extreme angle — casting cold green light across the canopy glass. Far below, 20,000 feet down through haze, the Persian Gulf coastline glitters with the cold blue-white dock lights of a concrete naval installation at Bandar Abbas — radar domes, communications masts, the dark silhouettes of frigates moored at grey piers — all rendered tiny and distant against the ink-black water. The sky is pure deep charcoal fading to void black at the top of the frame, no stars visible through the missile's smoke column. Color palette: dark crimson, charcoal black, burning orange, cold HUD green. High contrast. Oppressive. No text, no antialiasing.

dawn.driver: Scientists uncovered the nutrients bees were missing — Colonies surged 15-fold.
dawn.blurb: SCIENTISTS CRACK THE CODE TO SAVE OUR BEES
dawn.summary: In a world rattled by war and energy shocks, a quiet breakthrough may have just secured one of civilization's most critical foundations: researchers engineered a yeast-based 'superfood' that gives honeybee colonies exactly the nutrients missing from modern pollen, triggering a 15-fold surge in colony size. Bees pollinate roughly a third of everything humans eat, making their decline one of the most underreported existential risks we face. On a day when headlines are dominated by missiles and oil prices, this discovery reminds us that some of humanity's most consequential wins happen in a lab, not on a battlefield.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: A 15-fold surge in bee colony size from engineered yeast nutrition is a genuine breakthrough for global food security, not an incremental finding.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Pollinator collapse has been accelerating for decades; a scalable dietary intervention could reverse one of agriculture's most stubborn crises.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia signed a defense cooperation deal, showing that nations continue to build alliances even amid the Iran conflict's disruptions.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Nepal's 35-year-old former rapper Balen Shah was sworn in as prime minister on an anti-corruption mandate, embodying democratic renewal from an unexpected voice.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A stranded whale in Germany's Baltic Sea successfully swam free after a coordinated rescue effort cut it an escape route, a small but vivid symbol of humans choosing to help.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Ground-level view looking straight up from inside a Victorian glass greenhouse at Royal Botanic Gardens Kew, the towering iron-framed glass panes soaring overhead into a cathedral of emerald and gold, afternoon sun blazing through the greenhouse roof and casting dramatic hexagonal shadow patterns across everything below — the underside of a dense honeycomb frame held aloft by gloved hands dominates the upper frame, backlit amber-gold, each wax-capped brood cell glowing like stained glass, hundreds of perfect hexagons radiating warmth and life. Climbing the walls on either side, lush potted flowering specimens in deep emerald greens stretch upward. A small amber bioreactor flask sits on a shelf to the left, bubbling gently, its golden contents catching refracted greenhouse light and throwing warm prismatic color across the glass ceiling above. A feeding dish with pale supplement paste rests on the edge of an open hive box, visible at the periphery. Steam clings faintly to the upper glass panels, the air saturated and alive. Rich gold and vibrant emerald saturate every surface — golden honeycomb light filtering down, deep green plant life pressing in from all sides, the whole scene humming with quiet, patient triumph, 90 days of careful science culminating in this single glowing frame held up to the light.
